# Test Templates — Playwright Patterns for Journey Tests

Use these patterns when generating test files. Adapt to the project's
existing conventions (import style, fixture names, POM structure) — but
keep the journey-based structure and business-aware assertions.

## File Structure Template

Every generated spec file follows this structure:

```typescript
/**
 * @file [Journey name] — E2E tests
 *
 * Business flow: [Which journey from the flow map this covers]
 * Persona: [Which persona performs this journey]
 * Priority: [P0/P1/P2/P3]
 *
 * Generated by e2e-architect.
 */
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';
// Import existing POMs, fixtures, utilities as discovered

test.describe('[Persona] — [Journey goal]', () => {
  // Setup: auth state, seed data, preconditions

  test('[persona] can [action] [context]', async ({ page }) => {
    // Arrange: navigate to starting point
    // Act: perform the business action
    // Assert: verify the business outcome
  });
});
```

## Pattern 1: Sequential Journey (Steps Build on Each Other)

Use when a journey's steps must happen in order (e.g., onboarding).

```typescript
test.describe('Admin onboarding journey', () => {
  test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' });

  test('new admin can register with email and password', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/register');
    await page.getByLabel('Email').fill('test@example.com');
    await page.getByLabel('Password').fill('SecurePass123!');
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /sign up|register/i }).click();

    // Business outcome: account exists and user is redirected to onboarding
    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/onboarding|setup|welcome/);
  });

  test('new admin can create their first workspace', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/onboarding'); // or wherever previous step left off
    await page.getByLabel(/workspace name|team name/i).fill('Acme Corp');
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /create|save/i }).click();

    // Business outcome: workspace exists and admin lands on workspace dashboard
    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/workspaces\/|dashboard/);
    await expect(page.getByText('Acme Corp')).toBeVisible();
  });
});
```

## Pattern 2: Independent Journey Steps (Parallel-Safe)

Use when journey steps don't depend on each other (e.g., CRUD operations).

```typescript
test.describe('Admin project management', () => {
  // Each test is independent — uses pre-seeded data

  test('admin can add a new section to their project', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/projects/1/edit');
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /add section/i }).click();
    await page.getByLabel(/section name/i).fill('Backlog');
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i }).click();

    // Business outcome: section appears in project editor AND on public page
    await expect(page.getByText('Backlog')).toBeVisible();
  });

  test('admin can edit an existing task due date', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/projects/1/edit');
    await page.getByText('Design review').click();
    await page.getByLabel(/due date/i).clear();
    await page.getByLabel(/due date/i).fill('2025-04-01');
    await page.getByRole('button', { name: /save/i }).click();

    // Business outcome: date change persists after page reload
    await page.reload();
    await expect(page.getByText('2025-04-01')).toBeVisible();
  });
});
```

## Pattern 3: Cross-Persona Verification

Use when a business outcome must be verified from a different persona's perspective.

```typescript
test('published project is visible to viewers', async ({ browser }) => {
  // Step 1: Admin publishes the project
  const adminContext = await browser.newContext({
    storageState: '.auth/admin.json',
  });
  const adminPage = await adminContext.newPage();
  await adminPage.goto('/projects/1');
  await adminPage.getByRole('button', { name: /publish/i }).click();
  await expect(adminPage.getByText(/published/i)).toBeVisible();

  // Step 2: Viewer verifies the project is accessible
  const viewerContext = await browser.newContext(); // anonymous
  const viewerPage = await viewerContext.newPage();
  await viewerPage.goto('/p/my-project'); // public-facing URL
  await expect(viewerPage.getByText('Product Name')).toBeVisible();
  await expect(viewerPage.getByText('$29.99')).toBeVisible();

  await adminContext.close();
  await viewerContext.close();
});
```

## Pattern 4: Business Rule Enforcement

Use when testing that a constraint/limit is enforced correctly.

```typescript
test('free plan user cannot create a second project', async ({ page }) => {
  // Precondition: user already has 1 project (from seed data)
  await page.goto('/projects');

  // Attempt to create another
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /new project|add project/i }).click();

  // Business outcome: upgrade prompt appears instead of creation form
  await expect(page.getByText(/upgrade|plan limit|maximum/i)).toBeVisible();
  // Verify we did NOT land on a creation form
  await expect(page.getByLabel(/project name/i)).not.toBeVisible();
});
```

## Pattern 5: Error Recovery Flow

Use when testing that the product handles failures gracefully.

```typescript
test('suspended user sees reactivation path', async ({ page }) => {
  // Login as suspended user (use pre-seeded persona)
  await page.goto('/dashboard');

  // Business outcome: not a dead end — clear path to reactivation
  await expect(page.getByText(/suspended|inactive/i)).toBeVisible();
  await expect(
    page.getByRole('link', { name: /reactivate|contact|upgrade/i })
  ).toBeVisible();

  // Negative check: suspended user cannot access protected features
  await page.goto('/projects');
  await expect(page).not.toHaveURL(/projects/);
});
```

## Adapting to Existing Infrastructure

Before generating tests, check what the project already has:

**If POMs exist:** Import and use them instead of inline locators.
```typescript
import { ProjectEditorPage } from '../poms/ProjectEditorPage';

test('admin can publish project', async ({ page }) => {
  const editor = new ProjectEditorPage(page);
  await editor.goto('project-1');
  await editor.publish();
  // ... assert business outcome
});
```

**If test utilities exist:** Use them for common operations.
```typescript
import { loginAs, expectToast, resetSeed } from '../utils';

test.beforeEach(async ({ request }) => {
  await resetSeed(request);
});
```

**If persona fixtures exist:** Use them for auth state.
```typescript
import { personas } from '../fixtures/personas';

test.use({ storageState: personas.admin.storageState });
```

**If none exist:** Write standalone tests with inline helpers. Keep them
simple — don't create infrastructure for a single test file. Infrastructure
emerges from repeated patterns across multiple test files.
